theories.title: Iran War Ceasefire Completely Fraudulent
theories.summary: Multiple sources confirm the supposed Iran ceasefire is performative theater with continued ballistic missile attacks, Israeli escalations in Lebanon, and explicit threats. The 100-day mark shows both sides ramping military operations while pretending negotiations exist.
theories.why_signal: Coordinated reporting across financial, geopolitical, and investigative outlets with explicit 'this isn't a ceasefire' framing signals consensus crack in official narrative.

posts.title: 100 Day Mark: Iran Threatens Renewed Attacks On US-Israeli Bases, Citing IDF Escalation In Lebanon
Stop Calling It a Ceasefire
Iran War: Hezbollah Rejects Israel-Favoring Ceasefire, Casting More Doubt on Deal

theories.title: Tech Stock Crash Signals Market Decoupling Reality
theories.summary: Nasdaq suffered its largest single-day point drop on record with $1.8T wipeout as investors suddenly flee tech stocks. Financial commentators explicitly note AI bubble deflation and that companies aren't actually profiting from expensive energy costs despite talking about them.
theories.why_signal: Rare alignment between retail traders, financial press, and macro analysts on market dysfunction; admission that earnings don't match valuation signals structural break.

posts.title: S&P 500 sees $1.8 trillion wipeout, Nasdaq tallies biggest point drop on record
S&P 500 companies can't stop talking about higher oil prices. But few say they'll actually hit profits.
Who Needs Bitcoin & Cryptos (-50%) Amid the Semiconductor & Miracle IPO Mania?

theories.title: AI Systems Failing at Core Tasks, Widespread
theories.summary: Evidence emerges that AI systems cannot perform basic functions reliably: Meta's AI support bot was exploited to hack Instagram accounts, Google engineers mock their own AI as overhyped, lawyers cite cases that don't exist using AI citations, and AI gun detection systems failed at a school shooting.
theories.why_signal: Cross-security and engineering communities confirming systematic failures in core AI tasks suggests hype cycle peak and potential collapse of investment thesis.

posts.title: Hackers Used Meta's AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
Google Employees Internally Share Memes About How Its AI Sucks
School shooting survivor sues AI gun detection firm after system failed to spot weapon

theories.title: Housing Crisis Creating Permanence Trap
theories.summary: Multiple posts reveal housing as both unaffordable asset ($2000+ monthly mortgages at all-time high) and impossible shelter. Retirement communities going bankrupt after massive down payments, luxury developments in debt, and housing speculation colliding with reality of wage stagnation.
theories.why_signal: Financial press acknowledging the choose-one paradox (shelter vs. asset) suggests institutional awareness that housing market structure is unsustainable.

posts.title: Choose One: Housing Is Shelter, Or Housing Is Just Another Asset In A Bubble Economy
Americans' Average Monthly Mortgage Payment Tops $2000 For The First Time Ever
We thought we found the perfect luxury retirement community, but it's millions of dollars in debt

theories.title: Government Infrastructure Surveillance Acceleration
theories.summary: ICE expanding facial recognition access to 1000+ agencies nationwide, police tracking AI criticism as 'First Amendment activity,' military using GPS as covert comms system, and CISA contractors leaking AWS credentials. Surveillance apparatus rapidly normalizing and integrating.
theories.why_signal: Multiple unrelated surveillance expansions reported simultaneously across different government agencies suggests coordinated infrastructure push or norm shift.

posts.title: ICE's Plan to Let Cops Around the Country Scan Faces to Verify Immigration Status
The U.S. Military Quietly Turned GPS Into a Global 'Numbers Station'
Philly Cops Admit That They're Tracking 'First Amendment Activity' Critical of AI

theories.title: Democratic Party Structural Collapse In Key Races
theories.summary: Scandal-ridden Maine Democratic Senate candidate (Platner) facing Nazi tattoo and misconduct allegations yet party still backing him; Iowa Republicans unexpectedly strong despite Trump endorsement losses; Democrats 'too bureaucratic' and losing rural trust. Party establishment appears unable to manage candidate quality or respond to voter sentiment.
theories.why_signal: Party insiders publicly criticizing their own organization structure while backing imploding candidates suggests deeper institutional dysfunction than tactical losses.

posts.title: Democrats are furious after latest Platner revelations
Warner: Democrats have been 'way too bureaucratic'
The three things Democrats must do to regain rural America's trust

theories.title: Executive Overreach Beyond Vetting or Congressional Limits
theories.summary: Trump administration bypassing standard vetting (Pulte as DNI questioned as unqualified), distributing $620M loans to Trump Jr's company via White House pressure, ending police reform consent decrees, and defending 'anti-weaponization' funds. Institutional guardrails appear non-functional.
theories.why_signal: Investigative outlets documenting pattern of executive actions that bypass normal review processes suggests deliberate institutional circumvention rather than individual errors.

posts.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers After the White House Initiated a $620M Loan to a Firm Tied to Donald Trump Jr.
Senate Republican casts doubt on Pulte as long-term DNI pick
After the Trump DOJ Halted Police Reform, This City Stepped In. Then Officers Shot and Killed Katelyn Hall.

theories.title: Geopolitical Energy Shock Cascading Through Economy
theories.summary: Airlines cutting 2026 profit forecasts due to $100B+ jet fuel bill from Iran war; Texas BBQ restaurants shutting down due to beef shortages; UN warning millions pushed into hunger. Energy shocks are no longer theoretical but manifesting in supply chain collapse and business closures.
theories.why_signal: Real-world business and food supply failures directly attributed to geopolitical energy costs suggests sustained war premium is no longer marginal but structural to economy.

posts.title: Air fare rises 'inevitable' as airlines face extra $100bn jet fuel bill this year
'Texas BBQ spots are shutting down' as Trump's war contributes to beef shortages
UN Food Agency Warns Millions Pushed Into Hunger By Prolonged Iran War

theories.title: Mainstream Media Institutional Crisis Deepening
theories.summary: CBS News 'on fire' per fired 60 Minutes correspondent Scott Pelley; editorial management accused of interfering with reporting on police shooting; press and journalists publicly attacking their own organizations' editorial integrity. Trust in institutional media visibly deteriorating from inside.
theories.why_signal: Current and former senior journalists making public accusations of institutional interference signals breakdown in professional norms that would normally be contained.

posts.title: Pelley says CBS News 'is on fire'
Scott Pelley says Bari Weiss wanted 60 Minutes to say Renee Good was 'driving toward officer'
CBS legend spills on 'jaw-dropping' meeting before firing: 'Accuses me of physical abuse'

theories.title: Wealth Inequality Creating Retirement Safety Collapse
theories.summary: Multiple posts about elderly (ages 70-75) with $1.5M+ assets still worried about cash adequacy, Medicare premium triggers, and whether to leave money to adult children. High-net-worth retirees experiencing scarcity mindset suggests even wealth concentration insufficient for healthcare/longevity costs.
theories.why_signal: Single community but cluster of identical anxiety patterns in wealthy demographic suggests underlying cost structure has broken even high-net-worth assumptions.

posts.title: My husband and I are 75. We have $1.5 million in stocks and $425,000 in savings. Is that too much cash?
'This would be a one-time event': How can I take extra money from my 401(k) without triggering higher Medicare premiums?
